medicines.name: Paracetamol
medicines.mechanismOfAction: Inhibits prostaglandin synthesis in CNS by blocking COX enzymes, reducing pain and fever

clinicalUseDetails.details: Acts centrally on hypothalamic heat-regulating center to reduce fever. Safe for all age groups including children and elderly. Minimal GI side effects make it ideal for OPD use.
Inhibits CNS prostaglandin synthesis without peripheral anti-inflammatory effects. Suitable for patients who cannot tolerate NSAIDs due to gastric issues or bleeding risk.

medicines.medicineAdvantage: Effective symptom control with minimal gastric side effects. Safe for children, pregnant women (Category B), and patients with peptic ulcer history. First-line choice for fever and mild pain in OPD settings.

medicines.name: Ibuprofen
medicines.mechanismOfAction: Non-selective COX-1 and COX-2 inhibitor that reduces prostaglandin synthesis, providing anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antipyretic effects

clinicalUseDetails.details: Dual COX-1 and COX-2 inhibition provides potent anti-inflammatory action. Reduces prostaglandin-mediated inflammation at injury site. Best taken with food to minimize gastric irritation.
Effective for both osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis symptom management. Provides better pain relief than paracetamol for inflammatory joint conditions.

medicines.medicineAdvantage: Superior anti-inflammatory action compared to paracetamol. Ideal for musculoskeletal injuries, arthritis, and dysmenorrhea. Short-term use safe for most adults.
